text.title: devops-platform-golden-paths Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for devops-platform-golden-paths. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every golden path MUST explicitly document: (a) what it provides automatically (pre-configured items), (b) what the developer must supply as input parameters, and (c) the escape hatch — how to proceed outside the golden path without platform team involvement. Golden paths without escape hatches create the same bottleneck they replace.
rule.rationale: Developers only adopt golden paths when they trust they can leave them. Clear escape hatches signal that the platform respects developer autonomy, which drives voluntary adoption above mandatory adoption rates by 30-40% in surveyed orgs.

rule.statement: Backstage Scaffolder templates MUST include exactly four step types in order: (1) fetch:template — render skeleton files with developer-supplied parameters; (2) publish:github or publish:gitlab — create the repository with correct permissions; (3) catalog:register — automatically register the new service in the Backstage catalog; (4) optional notification step. Missing the catalog:register step means every golden path creates a service invisible to the service catalog, defeating the IDP's discoverability goal.
rule.rationale: Services not in the catalog cannot be discovered, have no ownership tracking, and cannot be included in dependency maps. Catalog registration must be automated — manual registration has near-zero compliance in practice.
